heading: Transitional housing assistance grants for victims of domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking
label: 12351 Transitional housing assistance grants for victims of domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section. Some section numbers or references in amendment notes below reflect the classification of such sections or references prior to editorial reclassification.section 13975 of Title 42

children.text: who are homeless, or in need of transitional housing or other housing assistance, as a result of a situation of domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking; and
for whom emergency shelter services or other crisis intervention services are unavailable or insufficient.
transitional housing, including funding for the operating expenses of newly developed or existing transitional housing.1So in original. The period probably should be a semicolon.1

short-term housing assistance, including rental or utilities payments assistance and assistance with related expenses such as payment of security deposits and other costs incidental to relocation to transitional housing for persons described in subsection (a); and

children.label: A
children.text: locate and secure permanent housing;

children.label: B
children.text: secure employment, including obtaining employment counseling, occupational training, job retention counseling, and counseling concerning re-entry in to the workforce; and2So in original. Probably should be “into”.2

children.label: C
children.text: integrate into a community by providing that minor, adult, or dependent with services, such as transportation, counseling, child care services, case management, and other assistance. Participation in the support services shall be voluntary. Receipt of the benefits of the housing assistance described in paragraph (2) shall not be conditioned upon the participation of the youth, adults, or their dependents in any or all of the support services offered them.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), a minor, an adult, or a dependent, who receives assistance under this section shall receive that assistance for not more than 24 months.

children.label: A
children.text: has made a good-faith effort to acquire permanent housing; and

children.label: B
children.text: has been unable to acquire permanent housing.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each eligible entity desiring a grant under this section shall submit an application to the Attorney General at such time, in such manner, and accompanied by such information as the Attorney General may reasonably require.

children.label: A
children.text: describe the activities for which assistance under this section is sought;

children.label: B
children.text: provide assurances that any supportive services offered to participants in programs developed under subsection (b)(3) are voluntary and that refusal to receive such services shall not be grounds for termination from the program or eviction from the victim’s housing; and

children.label: C
children.text: provide such additional assurances as the Attorney General determines to be essential to ensure compliance with the requirements of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: victims to participate in the criminal justice system in order to receive services; or

children.label: B
children.text: domestic violence advocates to breach client confidentiality.

children.heading: Report to the Attorney General
children.label: e Report to the Attorney General
A
children.text: the number of minors, adults, and dependents assisted under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: the types of housing assistance and support services provided under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the purpose and amount of housing assistance provided to each minor, adult, or dependent, assisted under this section and the reason for that assistance;

children.label: B
children.text: the number of months each minor, adult, or dependent, received assistance under this section;

children.label: i
children.text: were eligible to receive assistance under this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: were not provided with assistance under this section solely due to a lack of available housing;

children.label: D
children.text: the type of support services provided to each minor, adult, or dependent, assisted under this section; and

children.label: E
children.text: the client population served and the number of individuals requesting services that the transitional housing program is unable to serve as a result of a lack of resources.

children.heading: Reporting requirement
children.label: 1 Reporting requirement
children.text: The Attorney General, with the Director of the Violence Against Women Office, shall prepare and submit to the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives and the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate a report that contains a compilation of the information contained in the report submitted under subsection (e) of this section not later than 1 month after the end of each even-numbered fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: the Office of Community Planning and Development at the United States Department of Housing and Urban Development; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Office of Women’s Health at the United States Department of Health and Human Services.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $35,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), unless all qualified applications submitted by any States, units of local government, Indian tribes, or organizations within a State for a grant under this section have been funded, that State, together with the grantees within the State (other than Indian tribes), shall be allocated in each fiscal year, not less than 0.75 percent of the total amount appropriated in the fiscal year for grants pursuant to this section.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: The United States Virgin Islands, American Samoa, Guam, and the Northern Mariana Islands shall each be allocated not less than 0.5 percent of the total amount appropriated in the fiscal year for grants pursuant to this section.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: I .—In general
children.text: Not less than 10 percent of the total amount available under this section for each fiscal year shall be available for grants under the program authorized by .section 10452 of this title

children.heading: .—Applicability of part
children.label: II .—Applicability of part
children.text: The requirements of this section shall not apply to funds allocated for the program described in subclause (I).3So in original. Probably should be “section.—”.3

children.label: ii
children.text: Priority shall be given to projects developed under subsection (b) that primarily serve underserved populations.

children.label: i
children.text: has been submitted by an eligible applicant;

children.label: I
children.text: background checks of victims; or

children.label: II
children.text: clinical evaluations to determine eligibility for services;

children.label: iii
children.text: reflects an understanding of the dynamics of domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking; and

children.label: iv
children.text: does not propose prohibited activities, including mandatory services for victims.
